danger_signals.visual_form: Various animated enemy formations moving toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats requiring avoidance or destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: 2D environmental barriers and obstacles in scrolling path
danger_signals.meaning: Hazards to navigate around during horizontal progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinctly designed adversary sprites at level endpoints
danger_signals.meaning: Major progression gate requiring defeat to advance

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible visual items dropped by defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Increased firepower and combat capability available

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple colored bells with distinct visual properties
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary beneficial effects: invulnerability, point multipliers, special weapons (megaphone)

safety_signals.visual_form: On-screen gauge visualization lighting up from collected items
safety_signals.meaning: Character readiness for enhanced attack patterns

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Octopus protagonist Takohiko and selectable character roster
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Quest-driven level progression themed around takoyaki acquisition

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Various themed enemy groups matching level aesthetics
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different visual designs per level theme (disco-themed level enemies, etc.)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Level-specific enemy aesthetics reflecting parody targets

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Thematically-driven level environments reflecting parody of other Konami titles and Japanese culture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous horizontal scrolling progression with enemy squadrons appearing in waves

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Power gauge illumination on HUD; character firepower options expand visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enhanced attack patterns and multi-angle firing capabilities activated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Disco-themed visual styling (documented example), fast-food theming (Xexex parody), and other Konami game parodies (Ganbare Goemon, Tokimeki Memorial, TwinBee)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy and environmental behavior reflects thematic context; enemies styled to match parodied games
Game halts progression; player offered retry or quit option based on remaining credits

environmental_storytelling.example: Disco-themed level visually communicates context; fast-food themed level based on Xexex (1991) references Konami game library
Missing characters from previous Parodius titles (Goemon, Michael the pig) replaced with similar-moveset characters, maintaining visual consistency while updating roster
Takohiko protagonist framing establishes story context; 128-year takoyaki acquisition frames progression through hostile territories

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Voice acting by Jōji Yanami provides real-time narrative context during gameplay
environmental_storytelling.meaning: Communicates world-state and progression significance through audio-visual combination

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard directional movement across 8-axis; basic firing pattern
Multiple/angled attack patterns; expanded firepower options based on selected character
Temporary special conditions: consecutive shot damage boost, megaphone weapon, invulnerability frames
Organized formation movement toward player; responsive to player position
Drops power capsules or color-coded bells; visual removal from play field

notes: Jikkyō Oshaberi Parodius communicates world-state primarily through thematic visual environment design rather than faction symbolism. The game uses parody as its primary manifestation method—each level visually references different Konami properties and Japanese cultural elements, establishing context for enemy and environmental behavior. The running voice commentary layer (SA-1 chip enabled) adds narrative dimension to visual state changes. Danger and safety are communicated through classic shmup conventions: enemy squadron density/positioning indicates threat level, while power-up availability signals offensive capability windows. The quest narrative (takoyaki acquisition) provides overarching context for progression, though political or factional dynamics are minimal—the game emphasizes comedic parody over world-state complexity.
